meta_title: For Founders: project management to Solve tutorial hell (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome tutorial hell. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Project Management to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop watching tutorials and start shipping. Use this expert-led project management checklist for AI builders leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell is a symptom of poor project scoping, not a lack of coding skill. To escape, you must shift from 'learning' to 'shipping' by breaking your SaaS MVP into atomic, revenue-focused tasks. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for execution, you can maintain momentum and build profitable AI products.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks consuming endless 'Build a SaaS in 10 Minutes' videos, yet your repository is a graveyard of half-finished boilerplate code. You aren't learning; you're procrastinating under the guise of research. The cycle of passive consumption is the silent killer of every promising AI startup.

The breakthrough happens when you treat your code like a business asset rather than a learning exercise. By implementing a rigid project management framework and offloading the heavy lifting to AI-driven tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you transform from a passive student into an architect of production-ready software. It's time to stop building demos and start building businesses.

steps.title: Define the Revenue-First MVP Scope
steps.description: Identify the one core feature that solves a burning problem. Ignore the fluff; if it doesn't lead to a user paying, it doesn't get built.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business idea: [Insert Idea]. Break it down into the smallest possible MVP feature set that provides immediate value. Output a structured PRD with clear success metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create --plan 'Create a markdown file named MVP_SCOPE.md detailing the core features identified by the architect.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Before writing a single line of functional code, map out your data schema to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS app that handles user authentication, subscription tiers, and AI request logging. Output as a Prisma schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Initialize a new project, install Prisma, and create the schema.prisma file based on the Antigravity architecture output.'

steps.title: Implement Atomic Task Execution
steps.description: Break your PRD into tasks no larger than 2 hours of work. This prevents the 'overwhelmed' feeling that leads back to tutorials.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Take this feature list and convert it into a prioritized Trello-style backlog with 2-hour task estimates for a solo developer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Create a TASKS.md file and populate it with the prioritized backlog items from the architect output.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Shipping
steps.description: Use Claude Code to build one task at a time. Do not move to the next task until the current one is tested and working.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, modular implementation for the [Insert Task Name] feature. Ensure it adheres to DRY principles and includes error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Implement [Insert Task Name] using the logic provided. Run tests after completion.'

steps.title: Systematic Quality Assurance
steps.description: Automate your testing to ensure that new features don't break old ones, maintaining your velocity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of unit tests for the current codebase using Vitest. Focus on critical path edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Run the generated test suite and fix any regressions found during the implementation phase.'

pro_tips: Never start a session without a 'Definition of Done' for your specific task; it prevents scope creep.
Use Claude Code to refactor your own code every three days to keep technical debt at zero.
If you find yourself opening a tutorial, you are missing a fundamental architectural component; go back to Antigravity.
Prioritize auth and billing integrations first; if you can't collect money, you don't have a SaaS.
Commit code every 60 minutes to create a psychological feedback loop of progress.
content.next_steps: Once the MVP is functional, deploy to a production environment like Vercel or Railway and begin your first user acquisition campaign immediately.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of learning and start building for the sake of revenue. You have the tools, the architecture, and the roadmap. The only variable remaining is your execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
